🚀 Shipped — awaiting your verify 2

The AI reworked these — tick ✓ to verify, or reply that it’s still broken and it goes back to the AI.
💡 IDEA🤖 AI↩ SHIPPED · VERIFY#51
add a postpone button to a task when you push it will bring a list between 30mins to 3days
🔧 fix · b191: Added a ⏳ postpone row to the task pop-up (tap a task to open it) with quick offsets: +30m, +1h, +2h, +3h, Tomorrow, +2d, +3d. Postpone shifts the task to that new slot — from its existing scheduled time if it has one, otherwise from now — and nudges the due date along if it would fall behind. One tap re-slots it.
🔧 fix · b192: Added a ⏳ postpone row to the task pop-up (tap a task) with quick offsets: +30m, +1h, +2h, +3h, Tomorrow, +2d, +3d. Postpone re-slots the task to that new time — from its existing slot if it has one, else from now — and nudges the due date if it would fall behind.
💬 this shoud be for any task anywhere, if it already has a calendar slot then it should have postpone button on the task itself
🔧 fix · b212: Per your steer: the ⏳ postpone is now a dedicated button on the task row itself (next to ✎), shown for any task that has a calendar slot — not just buried in the date chip or the pop-up. Tap it and you get the same quick offsets (+30m … +3d) to re-slot the task. (The day-view already had this; now the lists do too.)

📝 NOTE#1281b210 · Settings · dev-i2dhz
verify ild 211 — deploy mtasks-211.html. Both in. Link a meal to an event. On the Diet page, the "Log a food" row now has a 🎉 link to an event field. As you type it suggests your upcoming dated items (so if Elena's birthday is on the calendar, it'll appear), but you can also just type it free-hand. So tonight: pick dinner, enter the cost/place, type/pick "Elena's birthday," and log it — the meal shows a 🎉 Elena's birthday badge in the day's list. If the text matches a real calendar item it also stores a link to it under the hood. Tested: the event saves on the entry and the badge renders. Settings toggle for action-needed items. Under Settings → Tasks & priority → "Show action-needed items" there's now a switch. Off hides the amber ⚡ banners that pin do-this items to the top of a page; on (default) keeps them. Verified the banner disappears when it's off and the toggle shows in settings. One honest caveat on the event picker: the suggestions currently include every dated task, not just social occasions — so business tasks like "Chase overdue invoice" show up in the list too, since the app has no separate "event" type to filter on. The free-text field means that doesn't get in your way (just type "Elena's birthday"), but if you'd like me to narrow the suggestions — say, only calendar appointments, or add a proper "event" flag you can mark birthdays with — that's a quick follow-up. And still queued whenever you're at the desktop: getting the server restarted onto build 200 (manual pkill + relaunch, then drop in restart-mtasks.sh), which also lights up the connection banner and makes the git-commit fix live.

✅ FIXED#1266b199 · TTR · Jobs · dev-i2dhz
russel hobs blender job should say how long it is til apptthis still isn't working, maybe explain better I might be looking in wrong place
🔧 fix · b201: TTR booked jobs now lead with a countdown to the appointment — the status line reads e.g. “📅 in 3 days · 30 Jun 2:00 pm” (or “tomorrow” / “in 4h” on the day). So the Russell Hobbs blender job tells you how long till the appointment at a glance. (Built on #1261’s status lines.)
💬 this istill needs fixing
🔧 fix · b205: The appointment countdown now shows on the job list itself (the On-my-plate / All-jobs rows), not just the expanded table — so the Russell Hobbs job reads e.g. 📅 in 1h right in the list. Also fixed which appointment it counts to: it now picks the next upcoming booking, not the earliest one (job 3 had past slots on 23 & 26 Jun, so it was reading “4d ago” instead of the 27 Jun slot).
✅ FIXED#1263b197 · Dev · dev-i2dhz
📸 Screenshot — Dev · 27 Jun, 04:39 am
💬 they dont get any bigger, the train tracks need to stay more or less the same, but the actual image need to bee able to take more area. infact whack a fresh bunch of unique image
🔧 fix · b201: Gave the pipeline a Matrix theme — digital-rain backdrop, glowing green nodes, mono labels — and a metaphor for the dev lifecycle: White Rabbit (the lead you follow) → Agent (the anomaly to defeat) → Jacked In (loaded into the running Matrix) → The One (proven, bends the rules) → The Source (back to the origin of all code). Every stage name is renamable — tap a label to rewrite it — and there’s a ▦ Matrix / ▢ Classic toggle under the pipeline. Also bumped the glyph size so the icon fills more of each node, and raised the canvas max-height that was scaling the slider’s growth back down.
✅ FIXED#1261b192 · TTR · Jobs · dev-i2dhz
appt booked will be , appointment booked for {insert time}, or parts due if parts are on order and the eta was entered, or if in progress thenthe last job note, or p&a sent 2 das ago, etc for all the job statuses, confirm with me the direction youre taking
🔧 fix · b198: TTR jobs now show a context line under the status, built from data you already have: Parts-on-order → “📦 parts due {earliest part ETA}” (from the parts ledger); Booked/Appointment → “📅 booked {date time}” (from a scheduled task linked to that job); In progress → “🔧 {your latest job note}”; P&A / quote / pending → “📨 awaiting approval”. That last one can read “P&A sent N days ago” once we capture a sent-date — say the word and I’ll add a one-tap “mark P&A sent” that stamps it. (This is the direction I confirmed; tell me any status→text tweaks.)

🔧 fix · b186: Split the post-handoff shortcut into two separate buttons. After you tap 🤝 AI Handoff and it lands, the right slot now shows 📋 Copy link and ↗ Open Claude side-by-side instead of one combined “Copy & Open” — copy the link, then open Claude as separate actions, in whatever order you like.
✅ FIXED#1245b183 · Dev · dev-i2dhz
the https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lofty-nz/mtasks-handoff/main/mtasks/build-xxx-release-notes.html needs to contain actual screenshot as images on the file if screenshot were taken
🔧 fix · b186: The handoff build-N-release-notes.html now embeds the actual screenshots. Any dev-note that has a 📷 screenshot attached renders it inline in its card in the report file — so the HTML is a complete visual record you can open in a browser. (One caveat: when I pull the report over the network I receive it as text, so to have me see a screenshot during a handoff, pasting it into the chat is still the way — the embed is for the file itself.)
✅ FIXED#1240b181 · Dev · dev-i2dhz
put the deploy button shortcut at top beside open source
🔧 fix · b183: Added a 📥 Deploy file button right under Upload Source on the pipeline. Upload Source grabs the newest build automatically; this one lets you pick a specific mtasks*.html / server*.py and deploy it — running through the same Matrix terminal view and reboot. So both deploy paths now sit together at the top.
✅ FIXED#1237b178 · Dev · dev-i2dhz
we need to have a way to clear the notificvations on the verifed list/icon this is done by pakaging all the fixes improvements etc into 1 and calling it a release on github. add the appropriate documentation that would accompany it to.
🔧 fix · b179: Wired the Verified list to clear via a Release. The Verified pipeline node now counts only items you’ve ticked but not yet released — so it’s a real “ready to release” queue (committing no longer silently sweeps them away). Hit 📦 Release build N (on the 🐙 GitHub panel header) and it now: bundles every verified item into that one GitHub Release, writes them into the release documentation (a “Included in this release” changelog of each fix/improvement + its note, on top of the build summary, with the release-notes HTML attached as a download), publishes it, then marks those items released so the Verified badge clears to 0. So: verify things as you go, and when you’re ready, one Release packages them, documents them, and clears the notifications.
✅ FIXED#1216b167 · Dev · dev-i2dhz
the server file shouuld be named with it build file from now on same arrangemant as the naming conventions for mtasks
💬 the server file should be called server_b1.py ....b2 b3 b4 etc in the program directory with the deploy /upload buttons automatically choosing the lates version (biggest file size) renaming it back to server.py for the actual running copy
🔧 fix · b180: From now on I’ll name the server file with its build number — server-169.py — the same way the app is mtasks-180.html. So server builds accumulate in your Downloads like the app builds, and Deploy newest picks the latest server*.py automatically. This round’s server is server-169.py (it’s the one that adds latest.html and powers Releases — still needs deploying).
✅ FIXED#1213b167 · Diet · dev-i2dhz
food tags need to be able to be clicked onto edit as well as to consume, from there you will edit price, supplier its qty or size a text entry details to make a note about this food,
🔧 fix · b180: Food chips are now editable, not just tap-to-log. Each chip has a small ✎ on the right — tap it to open an editor for price, supplier / brand, qty / size and a details / notes field. Those details are saved in a persistent food library (so they stick even if you haven’t logged that food in a while), and the chip’s tooltip shows the supplier, size and notes at a glance. Tapping the chip’s name still logs it as before — now using the saved price/supplier. (Next layer, if you want it: grouping foods into types — vege/meat/treats/drinks/takeaways — from #1203.)

🔧 fix · b163: Food entries can now carry a cost and a place/brand (e.g. $12 · Huntsbury Seafood). The chips remember them so re-adding a regular is one tap, the diet log shows today’s and this week’s food spend, Trends gets a food-spend sparkline, and the total flows into the Money page as a “🍔 Food & takeaways” line that counts against what’s left. ✓ DONE#1188b144 · Dev · dev-i2dhz
add a take photo button, when clicked allows you attach the photo to a block on the calendar it can be linked with tasks or jobs. and then that photothumbnail will show up on the calendar
🔧 fix · b189: Calendar blocks can now be linked to a task or a TTR job — the piece that was missing. Open any block and use the new 🔗 link to a task or job picker (task dropdown + TTR-job dropdown); the link shows in the editor with a clear button. Linked blocks carry a 🔗 marker in month, day and horizontal-bar views, and the day view shows the linked task/job name under the time. (The 📷 photo + thumbnail-on-calendar part already shipped back in b96 — this completes the “can be linked with tasks or jobs” half. Job list uses your local TTR jobs, same scope as the voice-note link.)
✓ DONE#1119b190
Voice notes on the home page: press & hold the mic to record, release to save, tap to play back. Assign a note to any task, asset, TTR job or contact. Saved in the data file so they sync. Needs mic + https (the Tailscale URL works).
🔧 fix · b191: Voice notes are already live — the 🎙 Voice notes panel on the Home page does exactly this: press & hold the mic to record, release to save, tap to play back, and the Assign button pins a note to a task, asset, TTR job or contact. They’re stored in the data file so they sync, and it works over the Tailscale HTTPS URL (mic needs https, which you have). If it’s not showing/recording for you, say what happens and I’ll dig in — otherwise tick it ✓.
🔧 fix · b192: Voice notes are already live — the 🎙 Voice notes panel on the Home page: press & hold the mic to record, release to save, tap to play, and Assign pins a note to a task, asset, TTR job or contact (stored in the data file, works over Tailscale HTTPS). Tick to verify, or tell me if it misbehaves.
✓ DONE#95b197
create a daily report page, where it displays uncompleted tasks for the day, provides a pie chart of how the days hours have been spent. and lists the days food and other intake, has a add button to add more, has a add button to add more tasks to the day and anything else you think should be useful, like an days income which adds up income portion for any jobs done that day + any time logged from ttr , calculate the total at $60 an hr (we can edit the labour charge out rate in the test tag repair settings area
🔧 fix · b186: Built the 📊 Daily report page — button on Today — outstanding tasks, an hours pie from the ⏱ timer, and the day’s food/intake, each with ➕ add. (build 184)
🔧 fix · b187: Added the 💰 Day’s income section to the Daily report. It sums the TTR time logged that day — from time entries on jobs and on job-linked tasks — and multiplies by your labour charge-out rate to show the day’s labour income, broken down per job/task. The rate comes from TTR → Invoice setup (the ⚙ on the Jobs tab; it’s the existing “default labour rate $/hr”, currently $90 — set it to $60 there and the report follows). Note: this v1 counts labour from logged time; if you also want parts markup or whole-invoice totals folded into the daily figure, tell me where you want “job done that day” to read from (parts ledger vs. invoice date) and I’ll add it as a second line.
💬 create a whole new tab for the home page, its going to be a fancy dashboard. its going to have shortcuts to the things i consume the most so i can add them a minituare bitcoin ticker, and we will add more, like a slideshow and clock later
🔧 fix · b196: Built the Dashboard — a new tab (the ⊞ icon at the top of the sidebar, under Home). It has a big live clock, a mini Bitcoin ticker (price + 24h change in NZD, pulled from your Markets feed), and a grid of shortcut tiles to the things you reach for most. Tap a tile to jump there; ＋ Add shortcut to add your own (point it at any page or a full https:// link); hover a tile and hit × to remove. Seeded with Today, TTR, Daily report, Markets, ChookNation and Calendar to start. This is the frame — a slideshow and more widgets slot in next.
✓ DONE#20b190
Warn / auto-avoid overlaps when time-blocking a task into an occupied slot.
🔧 fix · b191: The book-a-time grid already warned on overlaps; now it can auto-avoid them too. When your chosen slot clashes, a ⤓ next free slot button appears beside the warning — tap it and the start jumps forward to the first gap that fits the task’s duration (skipping every occupied block/booking that day). If the day’s full it tells you to try another day.
🔧 fix · b192: The book-a-time grid already warned on overlaps; now a ⤓ next free slot button appears beside the warning when your slot clashes — tap it and the start jumps to the first gap that fits the duration, skipping every occupied block/booking that day.

🏗 Recent build notes

b228 Branches explorer + switch that doesn’t choke on uncommitted changes
  • New 🌿 Branches section in the 🐙 GitHub panel — a visible list of every local branch with the current one marked ● current and a → switch button on the rest (next to the existing 🌳 Builds & releases tree). This is the “explorer” the dropdown was hinting at.
  • Switching no longer silently fails when you have uncommitted changes on disk (e.g. a just-deployed server.py): it now offers to commit them to the current branch first, then checks out and reloads. Git was refusing the checkout to avoid clobbering those edits — that’s why the dropdown looked dead before. Needs server build 206.
b227 Branch switcher in the GitHub panel — navigate branches
  • The 🐙 GitHub panel’s branch line is now a dropdown of all your local branches. Pick one and it checks it out and reloads onto that branch (the served files can differ per branch, just like roll-back-to-build). If you have uncommitted changes it warns first; they’re carried to the new branch, nothing is lost. Pair it with 🌿 Fork branch to spin up a branch and hop between it and master. Build navigation (↩ roll back / 🌳 build tree) is unchanged. Needs server build 205 (adds the branch list to /git-info).

🪵 Recent logs — deploy · handoff · git debug

The latest deploy / push output, newest first — included so the AI can spot errors directly.

Mtasks handoff · 1.2 · build 228 · flag older items 🤖 to pull them into Next-to-fix regardless of age.
